name: Marketing Company API
description: Minimalist GraphQL backend for CRM and marketing operations. Hexagonal architecture on Java 23 and Spring Boot, deployed on AWS (EC2, RDS, Upstash Redis) with external Kafka consumption and Prometheus-ready observability.
techStack: Redis (Upstash in production, local/Docker for dev)

problemStatement.problemTitle: Unified CRM and marketing data without a monolithic frontend lock-in
problemStatement.problemDescription: Marketing agencies and CRM teams need a single backend that models customers, sales pipelines, campaigns, and attribution — while staying API-first, secure, and deployable to cloud infrastructure without coupling to a specific UI framework.
problemList: Fragmented CRM and marketing data across spreadsheets and ad-hoc tools
Need for JWT-authenticated, rate-limited APIs suitable for multiple clients
Session and cache requirements that differ between local Docker and cloud (Upstash)
Operational need for health checks, metrics, and event consumption from managed Kafka

solution.solutionTitle: GraphQL CRM + marketing API on hexagonal Spring Boot

solutionList.title: Single GraphQL entrypoint
solutionList.description: One schema spanning account, CRM, customer, and marketing bounded contexts at `/api/graphql`.

solutionList.title: Hexagonal / DDD modules
solutionList.description: Ports and adapters per domain — GraphQL in, JPA/Redis out — keeping business logic testable.

solutionList.title: JWT + Redis sessions
solutionList.description: Access/refresh tokens with session state stored in Redis (Upstash in AWS).

solutionList.title: Cloud-native deployment
solutionList.description: App on EC2, PostgreSQL on RDS, Redis on Upstash, Kafka consumed from a cloud broker; Actuator exposes Prometheus metrics.

metricsList: 750+ Java source files across CRM, marketing, account, and shared layers
GraphQL API prefix /api with context path and Actuator at /api/actuator/*
Deployed on AWS — EC2 application tier, RDS PostgreSQL, Upstash Redis
External observability via Spring Actuator (health, metrics, prometheus)

layers.name: Input adapters (GraphQL)
layers.description: GraphQL controllers map schema operations to application commands and queries.

layers.name: Application layer
layers.description: Use-case orchestration — command handlers, query services, mappers.
components: Domain command/query services per bounded context

layers.name: Domain layer
layers.description: Business rules, entities, value objects, domain exceptions, and events.
components: crm (Opportunity, Deal, Quote, Task, Interaction, ServicePackage)
marketing (Campaign, Channel, Activity, Metric, Asset, AbTest, Attribution)

layers.name: Output adapters (persistence & external)
layers.description: JPA repositories, Redis session store, JWT provider, future Kafka listeners.

layers.name: Cross-cutting (config)
layers.description: Security, CORS, rate limits, logging, GraphQL wiring.

designPatterns.title: Hexagonal Architecture
designPatterns.description: Ports and adapters isolate domain from GraphQL, JPA, and Redis.

designPatterns.title: Domain-Driven Design
designPatterns.description: Bounded contexts for account, CRM, customer, and marketing.

designPatterns.title: CQRS (light)
designPatterns.description: Separate command and query types per use case without event sourcing.

designPatterns.title: Repository
designPatterns.description: Output ports abstract persistence behind adapter implementations.

designPatterns.title: Value Object
designPatterns.description: Immutable types (Email, DealStatus, UTMParameters, etc.) enforce invariants.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API tier
scalabilityStrategies.description: JWT + Redis sessions allow horizontal scaling of EC2 instances behind a load balancer.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Managed data stores
scalabilityStrategies.description: RDS and Upstash handle connection pooling and replication outside the app.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Connection pooling
scalabilityStrategies.description: HikariCP (max 20 connections) tuned for RDS limits.

securityStrategies.title: JWT dual secrets
securityStrategies.description: Separate access and refresh signing keys (`JWT_ACCESS_SECRET`, `JWT_REFRESH_SECRET`).

securityStrategies.title: BCrypt passwords
securityStrategies.description: Passwords hashed before persistence; plain passwords validated at domain level.

securityStrategies.title: Rate limiting
securityStrategies.description: Redis-backed limits on sensitive GraphQL operations.

securityStrategies.title: CORS allow-list
securityStrategies.description: Origins from environment; credentials enabled for SPA clients.

securityStrategies.title: Prod hardening
securityStrategies.description: GraphiQL off, reduced stack traces, SQL logging disabled in `prod` profile.

cacheStrategies.name: Redis auth sessions
cacheStrategies.description: Refresh tokens and user session sets
cacheStrategies.ttl: Session TTL (refresh token expiry, default 30 days)

cacheStrategies.name: Redis rate limits
cacheStrategies.description: Counters for global and per-operation limits

architectureFeatures.title: Schema-first GraphQL
architectureFeatures.description: SDL files in `graphql/` loaded automatically by Spring GraphQL.

architectureFeatures.title: Flyway migrations
architectureFeatures.description: Versioned SQL migrations; ddl-auto validate mode in production.

architectureFeatures.title: Profile-based config
architectureFeatures.description: dev, docker, prod, and test profiles in `application.yml`.

architectureFeatures.title: External observability
architectureFeatures.description: Actuator health and Prometheus metrics scraped from EC2.

requestFlow.title: Security filter
requestFlow.description: JwtAuthenticationFilter validates access token and loads roles

requestFlow.title: Controller → command
requestFlow.description: GraphQL controller maps input to application command/query

requestFlow.title: Domain + persistence
requestFlow.description: Handler executes domain logic via output port adapters (JPA/Redis)

requestFlow.title: Response
requestFlow.description: Mapped DTO returned as GraphQL data or structured error

eventFlow.title: External event published
eventFlow.description: Upstream system produces message to cloud Kafka topic

eventFlow.title: Domain handler
eventFlow.description: Event mapped to application command (integration boundary)

eventFlow.title: Side effects
eventFlow.description: Persist to RDS and/or invalidate Redis cache as needed

decisions.title: GraphQL over REST
decisions.problem: Multiple CRM and marketing clients need flexible reads without version explosion.
decisions.solution: Single GraphQL schema with modular SDL and Spring GraphQL controllers.
alternatives: OpenAPI REST with many DTO permutations
decisions.outcome: One endpoint, strong typing, GraphiQL for developer experience.

decisions.title: Hexagonal modules per domain
decisions.problem: Marketing and CRM logic must stay testable as the system grows.
decisions.solution: package-by-domain with explicit ports (input/output) and adapters.
decisions.outcome: Clear boundaries; swap GraphQL or JPA without touching domain rules.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis instead of self-hosted on EC2
decisions.problem: Session and rate-limit state must survive app restarts and scale horizontally.
decisions.solution: Managed Upstash Redis with TLS; configured via SPRING_REDIS_HOST in production.
decisions.outcome: No Redis ops on EC2; suitable for minimalist deployment.

decisions.title: AWS RDS for PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Need durable relational storage with backups for CRM/marketing data.
decisions.solution: RDS PostgreSQL 16; Flyway migrations on startup.
decisions.outcome: Managed backups and patching; app connects via JDBC env vars.

codeExamples.title: Auth command handler (application core)
codeExamples.description: Login flow orchestrates user lookup, password validation, token generation, and Redis session persistence — domain stays free of GraphQL/JPA details.

files.name: AuthCommandHandler.java
files.explanation: Core use case — validates credentials and creates session via ports.

codeExamples.title: GraphQL auth mutations
codeExamples.description: Input adapter exposing signUp, login, refresh, and logout with sensitive rate limits.

files.name: AuthController.java
files.explanation: Maps GraphQL mutations to commands; captures client IP and user agent.

codeExamples.title: Docker Compose host overrides
codeExamples.description: Full local stack forces postgres/redis service names so the app container never connects to localhost for data stores.

files.name: compose.full.yml (excerpt)
files.explanation: Environment block overrides .env localhost values inside the app service.
files.content:
app:
  environment:
    SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE: docker
    SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL: jdbc:postgresql://postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB}
    SPRING_REDIS_HOST: redis
    DB_HOST: postgres

codeExamples.title: Environment-driven secrets
codeExamples.description: JWT, database, Redis, CORS, and Kafka settings come from .env — loaded by spring-dotenv locally and passed to EC2/Docker in production.

files.name: application.yml (auth excerpt)
files.explanation: Production profile requires JWT secrets without insecure defaults.

codeExamples.title: Redis session repository
codeExamples.description: Refresh tokens are session IDs; user session sets enable logout-all.

files.name: RedisAuthSessionRepositoryImpl.java (excerpt)
files.explanation: Session keys prefixed with session: and indexed per user in Redis sets.

features.title: GraphQL API
features.description: Schema-first GraphQL with modular `.graphqls` files for account, CRM, customer, and marketing domains. Single endpoint at `/api/graphql` with GraphiQL in dev.
highlights: 22 schema files organized by bounded context
Global exception handler for GraphQL errors

features.title: JWT authentication & sessions
features.description: Dual-token JWT (access + refresh) with BCrypt passwords and Redis-backed session storage. Separate secrets for access and refresh tokens.
highlights: Access token default 15 minutes; refresh 30 days
GraphQL directives for authentication and roles

features.title: CRM domain
features.description: Sales pipeline management — companies, opportunities, deals, quotes, tasks, CRM interactions, and service packages.
highlights: Deal and quote workflows with line items
Task statistics and CRM interaction analytics

features.title: Marketing domain
features.description: Campaign management, channels, activities, metrics, assets, A/B tests, campaign interactions, and attribution tracking.
highlights: UTM and conversion tracking on interactions

features.title: GraphQL rate limiting
features.description: Annotation-driven rate limits with global and per-operation profiles, backed by Redis counters and exposed via response headers.

features.title: Redis sessions & cache
features.description: Auth sessions and rate-limit state in Redis. Production uses Upstash; local development uses Docker Redis or Upstash tunnel.
highlights: Caffeine in-process cache for app-level TTLs

features.title: Observability & health
features.description: Spring Boot Actuator with health probes, metrics, and Prometheus export for external monitoring on EC2.

features.title: Kafka event consumption
features.description: Consumes events from a cloud-managed Kafka cluster (AWS MSK or external broker). Bootstrap servers configured via environment — not hardcoded in repository.
highlights: Intended for async domain events and integrations

features.title: Docker local development
features.description: Multi-stage Dockerfile and two Compose files — full stack or infra-only — with explicit host overrides for container networking.

components.description: App + Postgres + Redis with service-name host overrides

components.name: .env
components.description: Secrets and connection strings — copied from .env.example

dockerFiles.description: Multi-stage build — Gradle 8.11 + JDK 23 compile, Temurin 24 JRE runtime.
dockerFiles.content:
# Build: gradle bootJar
# Runtime: non-root spring user, healthcheck GET /api/actuator/health
# Context: project root (..) when built from compose
FROM gradle:8.11-jdk23 AS build
...
FROM eclipse-temurin:24-jre-alpine
HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/api/actuator/health

dockerFiles.description: Full local stack — overrides DB/Redis hosts to postgres/redis service names.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  app:
    environment:
      SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL: jdbc:postgresql://postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB}
      SPRING_REDIS_HOST: redis
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile

dockerFiles.description: Infrastructure only — use DB_HOST=localhost and mapped ports for host-run app.

httpEndpoints.description: Primary API. Send JSON body `{ "query": "...", "variables": {} }`. Authentication via `Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>` header. Domains include account (auth, users), CRM (companies, opportunities, deals, quotes, tasks, interactions, service packages), and marketing (campaigns, channels, activities, metrics, assets, A/B tests, interactions, attribution).

responses.description: Missing or invalid JWT on protected fields

httpEndpoints.description: Register a new user. Returns access and refresh tokens. Rate limit profile `sensitive`.

example.query: mutation($input: SignUpInput!) { signUp(input: $input) { accessToken refreshToken user { id email } } }

httpEndpoints.description: Authenticate with email and password. Session stored in Redis.
Exchange a valid refresh token for new access and refresh tokens.
Public health and version queries defined in schema.graphqls.
Liveness/readiness for load balancers and external monitoring. Used in production on EC2 and in Docker HEALTHCHECK.

responses.description: UP when DB and Redis reachable

httpEndpoints.description: Metrics export for external observability stack (Prometheus/Grafana or cloud agent on EC2).
Interactive GraphQL explorer. Disabled in `prod` profile.
